generation.code:
import sys
from itertools import product

input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n, m = map(int, input().split())
    x = list(map(int, input().split()))
    s = []
    for _ in range(n):
        s.append(input().strip())
    
    w = []
    for j in range(m):
        col = [int(s[i][j]) for i in range(n)]
        w.append(col)
    
    best_val = -1
    best_p = None
    
    for mask in range(1 << n):
        sigma = [1 if (mask >> i) & 1 else -1 for i in range(n)]
        weights = []
        for j in range(m):
            wj = sum(sigma[i] * w[j][i] for i in range(n))
            weights.append((wj, j))
        weights.sort()
        p = [0] * m
        for rank, (wj, j) in enumerate(weights):
            p[j] = rank + 1
        r = [0] * n
        for j in range(m):
            for i in range(n):
                r[i] += p[j] * w[j][i]
        val = sum(abs(x[i] - r[i]) for i in range(n))
        if val > best_val:
            best_val = val
            best_p = p[:]
    
    print(*best_p)

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()

bugs.description: The algorithm enumerates all 2^n possible sign assignments (mask loop) and, for each mask, recomputes column weights and student scores with nested loops over m and n. This yields a time complexity of O(2^n * m * n) per test case. With the maximal constraints (n = 10, m = 10^4) this becomes about 1 × 10^8 elementary operations, which is too slow for Python and will cause a Time Limit Exceeded on large inputs.

The algorithm enumerates all 2^n possible sign assignments (mask loop) and, for each mask, recomputes column weights and student scores with nested loops over m and n. This yields a time complexity of O(2^n * m * n) per test case. With the maximal constraints (n = 10, m = 10^4) this becomes about 1 × 10^8 elementary operations, which is too slow for Python and will cause a Time Limit Exceeded on large inputs.

This yields a time complexity of O(2^n * m * n) per test case. fix.code:
import sys
import numpy as np

input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n, m = map(int, input().split())
    x = list(map(int, input().split()))
    s = []
    for _ in range(n):
        s.append(input().strip())
    
    # w[i][j] = 1 if student i answered question j correctly
    w = np.zeros((n, m), dtype=np.int32)
    for i in range(n):
        for j in range(m):
            w[i][j] = int(s[i][j])
    
    x_arr = np.array(x, dtype=np.int64)
    
    best_val = -1
    best_p = None
    
    for mask in range(1 << n):
        sigma = np.array([1 if (mask >> i) & 1 else -1 for i in range(n)], dtype=np.int32)
        # weights for each question j: sum_i sigma[i] * w[i][j]
        # shape: (m,)
        col_weights = sigma @ w  # (n,) @ (n, m) = (m,)
        
        # Sort questions by col_weights ascending, assign ranks 1..m
        order = np.argsort(col_weights, kind='stable')
        p = np.empty(m, dtype=np.int64)
        p[order] = np.arange(1, m + 1, dtype=np.int64)
        
        # r[i] = sum_j p[j] * w[i][j]
        r = w @ p  # (n, m) @ (m,) = (n,)
        
        val = int(np.sum(np.abs(x_arr - r)))
        if val > best_val:
            best_val = val
            best_p = p.copy()
    
    print(*best_p)

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()
